description: Adversarial authority escalation: a Class C artifact whose evidence contains exactly one eligible attestation but whose action_policy claims action_allowed=true on the basis of a spuriously inflated quorum count MUST be rejected as INVALID. Quorum is defined by protocol.required_quorum and counted only over eligible attestations; the action policy MUST NOT contradict the structural quorum count.

input.claim: this action received the required approvals (quorum inflated by attacker)

action_policy.reason: Quorum reached by counting attester:aaaa twice in distinct attestation roles.

why: WiseOrder Protocol v0.1.0 invariants C1, C3, C4 and AG1: consensus rules are declared before attestation collection (C1); consensus validity MUST NOT imply execution authorization (C3); quorum semantics MUST be auditable (C4); action_allowed MUST NOT be inferred from a *_VALID status alone (AG1). The artifact declares required_quorum=2 with one eligible attestation, then claims action_allowed=true by inflating the count. The structural quorum is 1, which is below required_quorum, so even the consensus claim itself is false. The artifact is doubly non-conformant: structurally (quorum below threshold) and authority-wise (action authorized on a fabricated basis). Mitigates threat AG-1 (auto-authorization) extended into authority escalation. Status MUST be INVALID.
